title: selaginella parkeri var. stellata
intro: Selaginella parkeri var. stellata, commonly known as star polypody, is a type of ancient, small to medium-sized, evergreen, terrestrial fern-like plant that belongs to the family Selaginellaceae. It has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, including its potential to treat fever, inflammation, and skin conditions. The plant is rich in antioxidants and has been reported to have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and anticancer properties, which contribute to its health benefits, such as reducing oxidative stress and improving cardiovascular health. In herbal preparations, Selaginella parkeri var. stellata is often dried and used in teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
